Gabriel Bonnin
Bio Projects Publications

Gabriel Bonnin , PhD

Postdoctoral Researcher · Lund University & Ruhr University Bochum

I am a clinical psychologist and researcher at the intersection of psychotherapy research, diagnostic assessment, and computational methods. As a Gateway Fellow at Lund University, I investigate how patient-generated language can be used for assessment and prognostic modeling in psychotherapy using large language models and NLP. I am also a licensed cognitive behavioral therapist and lead research groups within the German Center for Mental Health (DZPG).

Publications Projects CV

Research Interests

  • Classification of Mental Disorders
  • Long-term Effectiveness of Psychotherapy
  • Psychotherapy Research in Naturalistic Settings
  • Natural Language Processing

Positions

Gateway Fellow current

Lund University · Lund, Sweden

Feb 2026 – present

International research stay investigating patient-generated language for assessment, interpretation, and prognostic modeling in psychotherapy using AI and NLP.

Postdoctoral Researcher & Research Group Leader

Ruhr University Bochum · DZPG · Bochum, Germany

Jan 2023 – Jan 2026

Led the psychotherapy research group at the Bochum–Marburg site of the German Center for Mental Health (DZPG).

Education

PhD in Clinical Psychology 2024 summa cum laude

Ruhr University Bochum

Thesis: Implicit and explicit categorization processes in clinical psychology: Evidence-based diagnostics, optimal cut-points, and therapists' prototypes

MSc in Clinical Psychology 2018

Ruhr University Bochum

Thesis: Neural correlates of in-situ exposure in panic disorder with agoraphobia

BSc in Psychology 2015

MLU Halle-Wittenberg

Thesis: Physical activity and mood experience in daily life
